note: Dashboard exposes a `dashboard` config entity built with Layout Builder, a set of block plugins, dynamic per-dashboard permissions, and a login redirect. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using the contrib Dashboard module, what kind of thing is a dashboard, and where is its layout stored?
evals.expected_output: A dashboard is a `dashboard` config entity (config file dashboard.dashboard.<id>.yml) whose exported `layout` key holds Layout Builder sections; the entity implements SectionListInterface.

evals.prompt: How do I add blocks to a dashboard in the contrib Dashboard module, and which subsystem is used to arrange them?
evals.expected_output: Use the 'Edit layout' action which opens Layout Builder; add sections and drop blocks (Dashboard Text, Site Status, Placeholder, Views blocks, etc.) into regions. Depends on core layout_builder.

evals.prompt: I want editors and administrators to each see their own dashboard on this Drupal site. Using the Dashboard module, how do I give each role access to a specific dashboard?
evals.expected_output: Create one dashboard per role; grant each role the dynamically generated `view <id> dashboard` permission for its dashboard. getDefaultDashboard() returns the first enabled dashboard (by weight) the user may view.

evals.prompt: Where do I manage dashboards in the admin UI of the Dashboard module, and what is the configure route name?
evals.expected_output: Admin -> Structure -> Dashboard at /admin/structure/dashboard; the configure route is entity.dashboard.collection (requires the 'administer dashboard' permission).

evals.prompt: What permissions does the Dashboard module provide to control who can administer dashboards versus who can view a particular one?
evals.expected_output: Static 'administer dashboard' gates building/managing dashboards; a dynamic 'view <id> dashboard' permission is generated per dashboard to grant viewing access.

evals.prompt: With the Dashboard module, can a user be sent to their dashboard automatically after logging in, and what governs which dashboard?
evals.expected_output: Yes: a hook_user_login handler redirects to /admin/dashboard when the user has an accessible dashboard (unless an explicit destination is set or on password reset). The default is the first enabled dashboard by weight the user may view.

evals.prompt: Is this contrib Dashboard module related to the old Dashboard that used to be in Drupal core?
evals.expected_output: It is the contrib successor to core's old Dashboard module (which was removed from core); package is 'Core', depends on layout_builder, node, views, and targets Drupal ^11.2.

evals.prompt: Does the Dashboard module define its own plugin type, and what are its Dashboard-category blocks?
evals.expected_output: No new plugin type/manager. It ships core Block plugins (dashboard_text_block, dashboard_site_status, navigation_dashboard, dashboard_placeholder) and a Layout Builder 'dashboard' section-storage plugin (inline blocks disabled).
